SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.pump

Class PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.pump.PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Standalone pump hydraulics and net positive suction head (NPSH) calculator.

Computes pump hydraulic and brake power from flow, head, density and efficiency, and screens the suction side for cavitation by comparing the available NPSH against the required NPSH:

  • NPSHa = (p_suction - p_vapour) · 1e5 / (ρ · g) + static head - friction loss;
  • NPSH margin = NPSHa - NPSHr;
  • cavitation risk flagged when the margin falls below a threshold;
  • hydraulic power = ρ · g · Q · H, brake power = hydraulic / efficiency.

This is a companion screening calculator to PumpMechanicalDesign and the Pump.getNPSHAvailable() / Pump.getNPSHRequired() accessors.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator

      public PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator()
      Default constructor for PumpHydraulicsNpshCalculator.

  • Method Details

    • setDutyPoint

      public void setDutyPoint(double volumetricFlowM3H, double headM, double densityKgM3, double efficiency)
      Sets the duty point.
      Parameters:
      volumetricFlowM3H - volumetric flow rate in m3/h (must be > 0)
      headM - pump head in m (must be > 0)
      densityKgM3 - fluid density in kg/m3 (must be > 0)
      efficiency - pump hydraulic efficiency (0-1, must be > 0)

    • setSuctionConditions

      public void setSuctionConditions(double suctionPressureBara, double vaporPressureBara, double staticHeadM, double frictionLossM, double npshRequiredM, double marginThresholdM)
      Sets the suction conditions and NPSH criteria.
      Parameters:
      suctionPressureBara - suction absolute pressure in bar (must be > 0)
      vaporPressureBara - fluid vapour pressure in bar absolute (must be ≥ 0)
      staticHeadM - static suction head in m
      frictionLossM - suction-line friction loss in m (must be ≥ 0)
      npshRequiredM - required NPSH in m (must be ≥ 0)
      marginThresholdM - cavitation-margin threshold in m (must be ≥ 0)

    • fromPump

      public void fromPump(Pump pump)
      Populates the duty point directly from a NeqSim process Pump.

      Reads the volumetric flow, density and suction pressure from the pump inlet stream and derives the pump head from the pressure rise across the pump (head = (p_out - p_in) · 1e5 / (ρ · g)). When the pump exposes a positive isentropic efficiency it is used as the pump efficiency. The suction-side NPSH criteria (vapour pressure, static head, friction loss, required NPSH and margin threshold) are left unchanged so they can be configured separately via setSuctionConditions(double, double, double, double, double, double).

      Parameters:
      pump - the process pump supplying the duty point (must not be null and must have inlet and outlet streams)

    • calcHydraulics

      public void calcHydraulics()
      Runs the pump hydraulics and NPSH screening calculation.

    • getNpshAvailable

      public double getNpshAvailable()
      Returns the available NPSH.
      Returns:
      available NPSH in m

    • getNpshMargin

      public double getNpshMargin()
      Returns the NPSH margin.
      Returns:
      NPSH margin in m

    • isCavitationRisk

      public boolean isCavitationRisk()
      Returns whether there is a cavitation risk.
      Returns:
      true when the NPSH margin is below the threshold

    • getHydraulicPower

      public double getHydraulicPower()
      Returns the hydraulic power.
      Returns:
      hydraulic power in W

    • getBrakePower

      public double getBrakePower()
      Returns the brake (shaft) power.
      Returns:
      brake power in W

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the calculation results to a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the results
